articles.title: Doctrine Of Merger Vis-à- Vis Order 9 Rule 13 Explanation
articles.summary: The article explains the legal concept of ex parte decrees under the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 (CPC), where a court proceeding proceeds without the presence of one party (usually the defendant). It traces the historical background of such provisions and discusses the legal framework governing their setting aside. The discussion is relevant to India's judicial system and civil procedure laws.
articles.raw_summary: Code of civil procedure, 1908 (CPC) does not provide for the definition of ex- parte decree or ex-parte judgment. The term ex parte is a Latin term which means “from one side” or “or for one party only”. In other words, in an ex parte proceeding the case of the plaintiff proceeds in the absence of the defendant. articles.summary: A series of amendments to India's IT Rules has transferred control over online speech from the judiciary to the executive branch. This change allows for rapid, opaque content takedowns with minimal oversight. The Internet Freedom Foundation highlights concerns over accountability and free speech implications. The amendments mark a shift in how digital censorship is enforced in India.

articles.title: Police Cannot Arrest Accused In Complaint Case Unless Non Bailable Warrant Issued Along With Summons : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that police cannot arrest an accused in a complaint case unless a non-bailable warrant is issued along with a summons. The Court clarified that police have no power to arrest during an inquiry under Section 202 of the Criminal Procedure Code (Cr.PC). This decision restricts police discretion in arresting individuals during preliminary investigations.
articles.raw_summary: Police has no power to arrest even during the course of the inquiry under Section 202 of the Cr.PC, the Court added.

The court's directive marks a key step in the legal review of the Act.

articles.title: Legal Representatives Must Challenge Arbitral Awards Under Section 34, Not Article 227 or Section 115 CPC: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that legal representatives must challenge arbitral awards under Section 34 of the Arbitration Act, not under Article 227 or Section 115 of the Civil Procedure Code. The court clarified that the Arbitration Act's scheme does not end arbitration proceedings with a party's death, and Section 35 extends the finality of awards to parties claiming under them. This ruling impacts how legal challenges to arbitral awards are handled in India.
articles.raw_summary: Scheme of the Arbitration Act does not envision arbitration proceedings to cease with the death of a party. Section 35, Arbitration Act, extends the finality of an arbitral award not only to parties to the award, but also to  parties claiming under them .  The post Legal Representatives Must Challenge Arbitral Awards Under Section 34, Not Article 227 or Section 115 CPC: Supreme Court appeared first on SCC Times .

articles.title: Striped hyena gets stronger global protection
articles.summary: The striped hyena has been granted stronger global protection under the Convention on the Conservation of Migratory Species of Wild Animals (CMS) treaty. This decision was made at the 15th Meeting of the Conference of the Parties (CMS CoP15). The species' conservation now requires greater cooperation among countries where it is found, including India. The treaty aims to protect migratory species threatened by habitat loss and human-wildlife conflict.

MCX gold June futures fell 0.32% to ₹1,51,280 per 10 grams, while MCX silver May futures dropped 0.37% to ₹2,40,610 per kg. The decline is attributed to a stronger US dollar and higher crude oil prices, which typically reduce demand for non-yielding assets like gold.

articles.title: Polycotton Recycling Faces a Scale Test That Chemistry Alone Cannot Solve
articles.summary: The article examines the challenges of recycling polycotton blended textiles, framing it as an industrial systems problem rather than just a scientific one. Peter Mangnus of Avantium argues that solving this requires aligning feedstock, offtake, finance, and policy support. The debate underscores the need for regulatory and infrastructure interventions to address textile waste at scale. The discussion is relevant to India's textile sector and waste management policies.
articles.raw_summary: Blended textile waste is no longer being discussed as a marginal recycling problem but as an industrial systems challenge shaped by regulation, cost and infrastructure. In that debate, Peter Mangnus , Business Director Dawn Technology at Avantium , argues that progress will depend less on scientific novelty alone than on aligning feedstock, offtake, finance and policy support.

articles.summary: Justice Sharma of the Delhi High Court refused Arvind Kejriwal's plea to recuse from the excise policy case, stating that unsubstantiated claims of bias cannot be allowed to undermine judicial independence. The judge emphasized that the judiciary cannot be put on trial based on perceptions alone, setting a precedent for institutional integrity. The order underscores the court's commitment to maintaining public trust in the justice system.

The case highlights the use of preventive detention laws in smuggling investigations.

Locals view OPS as 'one of their own,' suggesting his switch may have significant electoral implications.

articles.title: 7 months after stampede, grief lingers in lanes of Karur while parties play blame game ahead of TN polls

The article notes the family's reluctance to engage with the 'Vijay factor' or political narratives surrounding the event.

The US Supreme Court's ruling against reciprocal tariffs necessitates recalibration of the deal to safeguard India's interests.

articles.summary: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has announced new regulations exempting online games without real-money stakes from mandatory registration. Business confidence remained high.

Processed food segments are also expanding, highlighting value addition's role in boosting exports.

articles.title: Shadow over Bengal polls
articles.summary: The article examines concerns over the conduct of elections in West Bengal, citing a messy voter list update (SIR) that has left many voters disenfranchised. It raises questions about the fairness and transparency of the electoral process. The issue involves the Election Commission and state authorities, with potential implications for voter rights and governance.
articles.raw_summary: A messy SIR has left many disenfranchised, raising questions about conduct of the election

articles.title: The political churn in Dravidian heartland
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu's political landscape is experiencing significant upheaval, which may have broader implications for national politics. The article highlights the shifting dynamics within the Dravidian heartland and suggests these changes could influence central government policies or political strategies. The focus is on the evolving political scenario rather than immediate policy actions.
articles.raw_summary: Tamil Nadu politics is in a time of great churn, and that will have a bearing on national politics as well

articles.title: Explained: How much US exposure is enough and when to rebalance
articles.summary: Indian investors rethink US equity exposure as valuations rise and rupee gains mature, experts advise rebalancing, diversification and goal based global allocations.

articles.title: Inside the TCS Nashik case: A deep dive into allegations, arrests and the unanswered questions
articles.summary: Eight employees of a TCS BPO unit in Nashik have been arrested for alleged rape and religious coercion. The case raises concerns about workplace safety, corporate governance, and gender-based violence in India. The allegations, if proven, could prompt regulatory scrutiny or policy reforms in labor and corporate compliance. No official policy action has been announced yet.
articles.raw_summary: The TCS BPO case in Nashik involves serious allegations against eight employees who have been booked for offences including rape and religious coercion.

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Is India's wheat export push a good idea?
articles.summary: India has reopened wheat exports amid a record surplus to ease excess supply and support farm prices. By offloading stocks, it aims to boost farmer incomes without stoking inflation, as ample buffer reserves and policy flexibility help keep domestic supply stable.
articles.raw_summary: India has reopened wheat exports amid a record surplus to ease excess supply and support farm prices. By offloading stocks, it aims to boost farmer incomes without stoking inflation, as ample buffer reserves and policy flexibility help keep domestic supply stable.
